lenses.guidance: vv 21-24's tithe to the Levites — the firstfruits and offerings as their inheritance — threads across Scripture into Heb 7:8-10's argument that Levi himself paid tithes through Abraham to Melchizedek. The chapter's economy is canonical scaffold.

vv 8-20's priestly portion — 'I myself have given you charge of my offerings' — frames priestly provision as covenant gift, not transactional wage. The priest receives because the LORD gives; the redemptive logic is recognition, not earning.
